**Top Recipe: One-Pan Lemon Chicken with Broccoli** [web:4]  
Serves 4 | ~30 mins  
Adapted from Farm Life DIY

- What to do (adapted to your fridge):  
  1) Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).  
  2) Toss broccoli and sliced bell peppers with a little oil and a squeeze of lemon juice (from 1 lemon). Season with pepper.  
  3) If you want to use the whole chicken, cut into serving pieces (or remove any skin you’d like). Place chicken on a sheet pan, surround with the broccoli and peppers.  
  4) Drizzle with a touch of lemon juice and roast until the chicken reaches safe internal temp and the veggies are tender (about 25-30 minutes).  
  5) Optional: finish with a quick squeeze of lemon over the top before serving.  
- Why it fits: uses chicken, broccoli, lemons; you can grill-roast peppers with the broccoli for color and flavor.  
- Source: [web:4]

**Alternative 1: Spiced Yogurt Chicken on Roasted Broccoli and Garlic** [web:1]  
Serves 2-3 | ~40-50 mins

- What to do (adapted to your fridge):  
  1) Mix a simple yogurt-lemon marinade with garlic, some salt/pepper, and your favorite spices (if you have them).  
  2) Coat chicken pieces with the marinade and let rest briefly.  
  3) Roast broccoli (and sliced peppers if you like) on a sheet pan with a little oil and lemon.  
  4) Add the chicken to the pan and roast until the chicken is cooked through and broccoli is tender.  
- Why it fits: uses yogurt, chicken, broccoli, lemons; peppers optional.

- Source: [web:1]

**Alternative 2: Lemon Chicken with Parmesan Roasted Broccoli** [web:3]  
Serves 4 | ~25-35 mins

- What to do (adapted to your fridge):  
  1) Toss broccoli with olive oil, lemon juice, and pepper; roast on a sheet pan until starting to brown.  
  2) Roast or pan-sear chicken pieces with a bright lemony glaze. If you have Parmesan, grate over the broccoli at the end for a quick cheesy finish (skip if not available).  
  3) Slice peppers and add to the pan for color if you like.  
- Why it fits: lemon + broccoli are core; peppers can be added; uses common pantry-friendly flavors.  
- Source: [web:3]

**Alternative 3: Broccoli & Red Pepper Frittata** [web: Olive Magazine]  
Serves 4 | ~25-30 mins

- What to do (adapted to your fridge):  
  1) Whisk eggs with a splash of milk, season with salt/pepper.  
  2) Sauté broccoli and red pepper pieces in a pan until just tender.  
  3) Pour in eggs and cook gently until set; finish under a broiler if you have one.  
- Why it fits: uses eggs, broccoli, and bell peppers; great for using produce and eggs together.  
- Source: [web: Olive Magazine]

Do you think I could add a sauce to the first recipe? What would you suggest?

[AI]
Yes—you can definitely add a simple sauce to the One-Pan Lemon Chicken with Broccoli using only what you have. Here are 3 sauce ideas using your ingredients (yogurt, lemons, peppers, milk). They’re quick and light enough to keep the dish balanced.

Option 1: Lemon-Yogurt Drizzle
- Ingredients (for 2 servings): 1/2 cup yogurt, 2–3 tablespoons lemon juice, lemon zest (optional)
- How to make: Whisk yogurt with lemon juice (and zest if using) until smooth. Drizzle over the roasted chicken and broccoli just before serving.
- Why it works: Bright, tangy, and creamy without adding any extra ingredients.

Option 2: Roasted Pepper Yogurt Sauce
- Ingredients: 1 red bell pepper (roasted or pan-softened, then chopped), 1/2 cup yogurt, 1–2 tablespoons lemon juice
- How to make: Puree or finely dice the pepper, mix with yogurt and lemon juice. Warm gently in a small pan if you like, then spoon over the chicken and broccoli.
- Why it works: Echoes the color and sweetness of the peppers plus a creamy tang from yogurt.

Option 3: Light Lemon-Milk Sauce
- Ingredients: 1/2 cup milk, 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- How to make: Heat milk with lemon juice over low heat until just thickened and slightly glossy. Spoon a thin layer over the dish or serve on the side.
- Why it works: A delicate, dairy-based lift that doesn’t overwhelm the lemony chicken.

Serving tips
- If you roast the peppers with the chicken, you’ll have extra pepper pieces to toss into any of the sauces for more texture.
- If you want a thicker sauce, use more yogurt and less milk; for a lighter sauce, use more milk.
